summary: Good‑quality 1973 audience tape captures a strong Dark Star → Eyes of the World stretch with Phil’s bass taking charge.
review: Both reviewers give this partial 1973 audience recording an average three‑star rating, but they single out the second‑set Dark Star into Eyes of the World sequence as a highlight, praising Phil Lesh’s prominent role. The tape is described as “very nice” for its era, with no major sound issues noted.

ai_rating.rationale: Two moderate‑rating reviews (both 3 stars) note good sound for a 1973 audience tape and highlight strong performances, yielding an overall rating just above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear for a 1973 audience recording; reviewers mention it is "very nice" with no significant flaws reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set contains a notable Dark Star → Eyes of the World stretch; overall flow is solid despite some missing tracks in the listing.

band_member_comments.Phil: Phil takes charge with prominent, energetic bass work during the Dark Star/Eyes of the World segment.
